title: The Interplay of Power, Sample Size, Effect Size, and Significance
description: A conceptual graph showing how statistical power is determined by the relationships between sample size, effect size, and significance level, and how it connects to Type I and Type II error.
relationships: Increasing Sample Size directly increases Statistical Power
Larger Effect Size directly increases Statistical Power
A stricter (lower) Significance Level decreases Statistical Power
Statistical Power is inversely related to Type II Error
Significance Level is directly related to Type I Error
